  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Shackle is side-loaded",
    "Side-loading reduces shackle WLL and can cause catastrophic failure"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately",
    "Release tension on rigging",
    "Remove and inspect shackle for deformation or damage",
    "Reeve rigging so shackle is loaded in line with bow plane",
    "Replace shackle if any distortion is found"
  ],
  "rationale": "A side-loaded shackle is loaded across the pin or bow in a direction not aligned with the plane of the bow. This induces bending and concentrated stress, reducing the safe working load well below the rated capacity. ASME B30.26 requires shackles be loaded in line with the bow plane. OSHA 1926.251 requires rigging hardware to be used in accordance with manufacturer recommendations and free from defects; side-loading violates intended use and constitutes a defective rigging condition.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.26 Rigging Hardware",
    "OSHA 1926.251 Rigging equipment for material handling"
  ]
}
